output.dependency: The textual title/description column appears to determine whether the record includes a clinical trial registration identifier. Records that look like interventional or hospital-based study titles can contain a value in the identifier column, while most review/guideline/meta-analysis titles have the placeholder null set. The identifier column also follows a specific pattern such as an NCT code inside braces when present.

The title/description column suggests publication/study type, and this sample shows a dependency where records that are reviews, meta-analyses, guidelines, or similar article types all share the same numeric flag/value of 0.0 in this column.

These two numeric columns are syntactically dependent in the sample: they always take the same value (0.0 paired with 0.0), suggesting duplicated status indicators, synchronized flags, or one derived directly from the other.
These two columns are jointly dependent in the sample because both are always null together, suggesting related optional attributes that are absent under the same conditions.
These columns appear dependent by missingness pattern: whenever the first is null, the second is also null in all shown records, suggesting a shared subtype or grouped metadata fields.
These columns appear dependent by missingness pattern: both are null for every shown record, which may indicate they belong to the same unused or conditionally populated metadata group.
These two columns have the same missingness behavior in the sample, always null together, suggesting related attributes where one would likely only be populated when the other is also relevant.
